texto: And I don't believe in such a thing as "happily ever after". There's only happily every now and then. I find the hardest trick is to recognize the now-and-thens, and to bask in them when they come. Happiness is a choice we make, like how to wear our hair, or having coffee with breakfast and tea at night.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cindy Bonner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “And I don't believe in such a thing as "happily ever after". There's only happily every now and t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, happiness, happy-endings pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
